analysis.name: Aidan Ouckama
analysis.verdict: Solid pick — well-liked with reasonable difficulty
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 3 reviews — take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: Immediately recognized him from his videos. LOVE HIM!!
Great dude! Really passionate about what he teaches.

analysis.name: Andrew Ng
analysis.verdict: Good teacher but expect a challenge
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 24 reviews — decent sample but could shift

review_highlights.comment: Made a wonderful subject unnecessarily hard because of hurried explanations and VERY sloppy notation. Every method has the term K, so K can mean any number of different things on a given day of the week. Sucks.

His lecture notes are very clear, but he's a terrible lecturer in person. He mumbles and writes equations on the board without differentiating useful ones from algebraic derivations. I'm very serious when I say that he draws arrows labeled "magic" when he gets tired of explaining things.

A great teacher who always explains hard-to-understand concept in a easy way. The programming exercises really helps me gain deeper understanding about ML. The course is hard, but Prof really really did a good job!

Prof. Ng is very polite but he rushes explanations and his notes don't distinguish scalars/vectors/matrices. He overloads the meaning of various notational terms. Homework is very hard (most TAs don't understand it) and requires you to know magic never taught in class. Great subject but absolutely ridiculous course.
Very well-organized class on a hard topic. Great lecture notes. Freedom to work on a cutting-edge project.

analysis.name: Alex Aiken
analysis.verdict: Below average reviews — check if this fits your learning style
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 3 reviews — take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: Has a lot of knowledge but teaches really badly. Skips over many of the basic details and just jumps to conclusions. The course content was boring and the teaching was even more awful. Had trouble just staying awake in the class let alone focus. Would not take this professor again.
Great teacher, tough assignments.

analysis.name: Janet Alexander
analysis.verdict: Mixed reviews — experiences vary a lot between students. And trending downward
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 6 reviews — take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: Prof. Alexander was one of my best professors in law school.  It's been many years since I was a 1L in her intro Civ Pro class but to this day, I remember her lectures, and even a particular  final exam question on Rule 19, that has been important to me years later i in practice. Really grateful I was in her class.

Had three courses from her when I was a student.  One of my favorites at law school because of her dedication and sincerity.  Actually made Civ Pro come to life for me. Fed Cts was during a difficult period for her so not as good as it could be.  A real gem for SLS!
Best professor I've ever had. Great insights into the material. Really enjoys talking to students outside of class. Definitely one of the law school's greatest assets.
it's not nearly as complicated as she makes it, and much more interesting than her class suggests
Take her if you want to learn absolute nothing and be ready to fail.

analysis.name: Marta Baldocchi
analysis.verdict: Solid pick — well-liked with reasonable difficulty
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 11 reviews — decent sample but could shift

review_highlights.comment: Marta's passion for teaching is evident: lessons are well-structured, she creates a positive atmosphere in the classroom, and she cares about her students.  Attend, participate, and do your homework, and you'll learn and succeed.

Didn't learn much. Few lectures: you're expected to read book / do exercises online by yourself. Bring questions to class. Little to no feedback from instructor. Half of each class is conversation. Students speak on topics of their choosing, followed by questions / discussion. Good practice, but leaves little/no time for lectures or new material.
Dedicated, caring and passionate about students learning.

She's the consummate language teacher: she demands copious participation and offers ample cultural context. A native of Milan and strict yet understanding in times of illness, she is perfect for her position and a great instructor.
Nice class: I learnt moltissimo!

analysis.name: Emma Brunskill
analysis.verdict: Most students had a tough time — consider alternatives if possible
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 4 reviews — take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment:
Her lectures have no organization or structure. Slides are chaotic and disorganized. She speaks fast; jumps from one topic to another; fails to provide any parallels, etc.
There are three HWs in the class, which cover the material very poorly.
Too large a class. Lectures not helpful. I'd choose to learn the theory part on my own instead of going to lectures and ending up learning nothing.
The course is simply insufferable and very disorganized.
She mumbles trough out the lectures ....

analysis.name: Justin Du Bois
analysis.verdict: Good teacher but expect a challenge. And trending downward
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 7 reviews — take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: Great prof who loves the subject and cares about his students. Lectures are great.

dubois is a good teacher, but he moves way too fast in this class; he skips over a lot.  Part of the problem is that there are (college) seniors to 3rd+ year grad. students (who are taking the class the 2nd time_, so there's definitely a big range of background.  Problem sets are hard; you will learn a lot, but it will be a lot of work!!
he is obviously very smart but that does not make him the best teacher. he is way too fast. even the hwk is difficult without TA help. not that clear.
The best orgo chem professor I've had at Stanford!

analysis.name: Noah Burns
analysis.verdict: Good teacher but expect a challenge. And trending downward
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 4 reviews — take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: Great prof. He's funny, lectures effectively, and answers questions really well.
This class was horrible, I have to retake it but I have NO REGRETS because Noah is life.
Tough class lead by excellent professors.
Noah is very passionate about organic chemistry and explains it very well and clearly. His office hours are enlightening and his dog is very cute.

analysis.name: Gill Bejerano
analysis.verdict: Most students had a tough time — consider alternatives if possible
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 4 reviews — take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: Really cool class, however it is poorly organized. Lecture and homework are divorced from one another with very limited resources for help. When asking questions, professor sometimes comes out a bit condescending. No real resources for final exam. Overall, course is conceptually amazing but poorly organized, and limited resources for students.

Very long psets, little help outside of class, harsh attitude when you go for help. Also not respectful of university policy for accommodations. Ultimately not a good professor at all, would stay away if you can avoid any class he teaches.
shitty lecture and hard grader. just a bad guy. do not take a class with him, please. i beg u.
Very condescending, and extremely unprofessional in his responses to student questions, also terrible at communication

analysis.name: Dan Boneh
analysis.verdict: Good teacher but expect a challenge
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 21 reviews — decent sample but could shift

review_highlights.comment: He's an incredible professor. It's truly a privilege to sit in his lectures.
Dan Boneh is the man.  That is all.
boneh is the freakin man.  that class was hard but he is such a genius and funny
Excellent lecturer, but homeworks are HARD
If you really want to pass , better read more about cryptography and prepare for hard tests and assignment. His lectures are amazing and clear. I understand cryptography more now.

analysis.name: Chris Piech
analysis.verdict: Great professor — tough but students love the teaching
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 26 reviews — decent sample but could shift

review_highlights.comment: Chris Piech is everything! A natural teacher who loves his material and gets students to love it too. I want to be all that, do what he does, live that amazing life of being a great teacher at the world's greatest CS department.

The most inspiring professor at Stanford. Brings in real material into every problem and is the most encouraging, approachable, funny lecturer - he encourages everyone to apply AI to benefit the world/social good.

Chris Piech is doing amazing things with CS109. HE's so clear, interesting and helpful.  The material is tough, but it's rewarding to do such interesting psets and have such great lectures.  He's the BEST!
An engaging and inspiring teacher. His lectures are clear and he makes you understand why learning the material matters.
A true blessing. A wonderful man and amazing instructor. Passionate about his work.

analysis.name: Stan Christensen
analysis.verdict: Great professor — tough but students love the teaching. And trending downward
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 11 reviews — decent sample but could shift

review_highlights.comment: Stan is an exceptional teacher. He brings thoughtful, grounded perspectives drawn from a wide range of experiences, making every class feel fresh. His approach to negotiation is not about tactics, rather frameworks for relationship building, judgment, and navigating difficult situations. You learn how to think and act better in everyday life. 10/10

Professor Christensen's course was engaging, insightful, and inspiring. He values participation, gives clear feedback, and encourages critical thinking. The class was moderately challenging but rewarding. I felt supported and intellectually stimulated. Highly respected and truly inspirational.

I took Stan's Negotiation class in 2016 and it was an amazing experience for me. Stan does a fantastic job of founding philosophical frameworks with pertinent anecdotal evidence in such a captivating speaking style, that you just can't help but be engaged! I also appreciated his challenges to speak up and voice opinions on class conversations.

I took Stan's Negotiations class in 2015. Stan is well-known to be a phenomenal teacher; he gets 200+ applications for 50 spots, with students applying across all majors and degrees at Stanford. Of all the classes I took in my 4 years at Stanford, this is the class I most frequently think about and reference in my day-to-day.

Stan is an amazing professor, very easy to talk to and always concerned about the the students. He taught 'real world' skills which I use everyday in my personal & professional life. I highly recommend any course taught by Stan esp. Negotiation (MS&E 285). I am sure people's life will change forever after learning from Stan.

analysis.name: Chris Gregg
analysis.verdict: Great professor — tough but students love the teaching
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 10 reviews — decent sample but could shift

review_highlights.comment: This is a pretty good teacher who lectures clearly and tries really hard to be nice. But he's defensive, assumes you already know all kinds of stuff to be in his class, then gets frustrated if what he said isn't totally obvious to you right away.

This teacher may come off as a bit conservative and rigid, but when you get used to his teaching style you see he's very consistent and wants you to be too. Super nice, really wants you to succeed and have a solid CS foundation.
106B is a great class and Chris Gregg filled in so many holes in my CS understanding. This is the key course to move forward, and this teacher totally gets it!
Awesome prof! Super clear, helpful, and sooooo patient and caring with students.
Great class, great professor, and entertaining lectures.

analysis.name: Jerry Cain
analysis.verdict: Great professor — tough but students love the teaching. And trending downward
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 55 reviews — fairly reliable

review_highlights.comment: Great teacher, great lecturer super clear and helpful!

Jerry always makes the class significantly harder. His lectures go very quickly, if you don't pay attention for 1 second, you're lost for the rest of the class. He's good at explaining, but I feel goes too quickly in lecture and doesn't stop enough to make sure students are getting it. Explanations could be less code heavy and more visual.
Jerry is the boss! Explains Computer Systems very clearly and really cares about his students.

I had thought that Chris Piech/Mehran Sahami were the best Professors -- but now, I think its Jerry Cain - he literally cares for each and every student, responds to any small question to make sure you get timely help - his lectures are amazing, very down to earth to help students succeed, course website/Ed is so well organized, love, love,love him
I've taught this stuff for decades and Jerry explained it better, and way, way faster, than I ever have. Inspirational.

analysis.name: Carol McKibben
analysis.verdict: Mixed reviews — experiences vary a lot between students
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 5 reviews — take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: Dr. McKibben is very judgmental and has a snobby personality, thinking she is better than her peers. Even though she is a very knowledgeable professor, she is not a kind person. Would not take this professor again.
She's disorganized and can be pompous and overly judgmental.

Professor McKibben is extremely thoughtful, knowledgeable, and kind. She took the time to work with every student to help them develop a research topic and made herself available throughout the quarter when students had questions. I liked that while she gave input, she still let students pick which roads to go down, meaning the did not micromanage.
A great and interesting class, but it was very unorganized. Papers were fairly graded, and the final was a couple of short, take-home essay questions.

Professor Mckibben is passionate professor with a student centered approach. Her assigned materials were interesting and useful if your researching or curious about California and the West. While in class discussions diverged at times, the overall course was interesting, I got feedback on readings related to my own work, which also was a plus.

analysis.name: Kurt Carrasquilla
analysis.verdict: Great professor — tough but students love the teaching
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 90 reviews — fairly reliable

review_highlights.comment: I highly recommend Professor Carrasquilla. Not only does he have a wealth of industry knowledge, but he communicates the material clearly and with enthusiasm. In addition, he makes himself as available as possible for students with questions, and is patient and intentional to any student that struggles with concepts. He's a top professor.

I took 'Essentials of Wealth Management' over the summer, which is a short five week class.  Kurt covered a lot of content in that time.  The course was well organized and Kurt was great at taking questions and stayed at the end of class for people to follow up if they wanted. If he taught a follow-up class at some point I would take it.

Having Kurt as a professor in "Essentials of Wealth Management" has been an incredible experience. I've had the opportunity to learn a lot from him about both basic and more advanced personal finance, and Kurt has also supported me with my personal projects to create an investment fund. Highly recommended.

By difficult, I do not mean hard to understand or similar. It is the information presented and the challenge given for a grade that motivates me. The more difficult it is, the more challenging and motivational it is

Mr Carrasquilla is a patient instructor that cares about his students. BUS 195 content will be greatly helpful to most families and individuals as well so highly recommend everyone to take this course.

analysis.name: Cynthia Lee
analysis.verdict: Good teacher but expect a challenge. And trending downward
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 13 reviews — decent sample but could shift

review_highlights.comment: This is one of the best courses I've taken at Stanford and Cynthia Lee totally understands all the issues, she has a vision of how Silicon Valley needs to change, but more importantly she's willing to hear all of our ideas. This is true leadership
Cynthia is an inspiration to women in CS! It's super hard to take on a new class, she's doing a great job making 103 more accessible and fair.

Cynthia Lee really goes the distance for her students. She cares about making CS education inclusive and works very hard to help students continue even when they feel overwhelmed by the competition and difficulty of the assignments and test.
Professor Lee is amazing! I'm so grateful to be in a systems course taught by a woman, who understanding how bad CS pedagogy has been in the past and how much it needs to change! She's my hero!
Cynthia Lee has done so much to make this very stressful, high stakes course CS107 accessible.  She explains the material well and offered a lot of preparation for exams and homework.

analysis.name: Eric Roberts
analysis.verdict: Good teacher but expect a challenge. And trending downward
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 22 reviews — decent sample but could shift

review_highlights.comment: Roberts is the master!  Great ability to explain ALL important computer science concepts you need to know! Be sure to talk to him after class --there's always more to the lectures.

rarely does a reputation live up to expectations, but Professor Roberts is truly amazing. I hope he doesn't completely retire, he's one of a kind professor who knows both arts and sciences. His books are amazing too
Reason why I understood recursion and algorithm!!

I took his "visions of utopia" introsem and it was tons of fun. there was a lot of reading, but eric is really cool and always structured class well. not to mention, he invited us over to his house to watch the movie of 1984 and eat a full home-cooked dinner. it was greatly appreciated, especially as a rest from dining hall food. he's just a really cool guy.
Eric Roberts is a great teacher, and his textbooks are top notch.

analysis.name: Michelle Elam
analysis.verdict: Mixed reviews — experiences vary a lot between students
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 4 reviews — take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: Great professor, very caring and prioritizes the students
Energetic and enthusiastic. She'll make you love the material.
Not a good lecturer, and not supportive of students.

analysis.name: Elizabeth Tallent
analysis.verdict: Good teacher but expect a challenge
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 3 reviews — take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: So true! She is AMAZING. Take an introsem with her!!

Prof Tallent is BRILLIANT, interesting, well-spoken, kind, and just entirely badass.  To take one of her classes is to love her, and also to learn so freaking much.  My adoration knows no bounds, and I know I'm not the only one who feels this way.
Expects a lot from her students in a good way

analysis.name: Erin Ferris
analysis.verdict: Mixed reviews — experiences vary a lot between students. And trending downward
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 4 reviews — take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: Our meetings really helped me get started writing papers. After meeting with her I knew what I wanted to say and I was excited to start writing (this NEVER happens to me!). I enjoyed our sections although I didn't enjoy many of the works we studied. She made IHUM a good experience for me, which given IHUM and especially this course is saying a lot.

LOVED her! She is absolutely amazing, the best TA I have had during my four years at Stanford. Extremely clear with her subject matter, all her relations are really insightful. I just cannot say enough good things about her. The only thing I've heard is that she's hard. I did well in her class, but I went to office hours to clarify paper topics.

I took her online class over the Summer and she wasn't really involved. When i submitted papers and assignments she would take her time in grading them and would give me little to no feedback on them. At the end of he semester she marked everything off with an A and sent me on my way. Not that I'm complaining about that, i just wanted feedback.
****, ****, ****.  No help with writing essays, no insight provided other than her own ideas which we cannot use.  Smoking, hateful, etc.

analysis.name: Dan Edelstein
analysis.verdict: Solid pick — well-liked with reasonable difficulty
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 7 reviews — take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: I must second the previous two opinions; what a delightful man. What a great teacher - so helpful, so patient. His humor is so sweetly funny - nothing sarcastic about it (maybe he's being extra-gentle with the undergrads).  Adorable, adorable, adorable.
Professor Edelstein is a joy to learn under. His lectures are engaging, and he is easy to approach outside of class. I highly recommend Professor Edelstein to any prospective students!
Adorable.  Bright, funny, fuzzy-haired, polymathic, informal, w/kind smile.
Best ESF class, great discussions, adorable guy with cutest dog
Kind of funny, but his lectures where a load of****. I like it when he played the piano.

analysis.name: Fouad Tobagi
analysis.verdict: Most students had a tough time — consider alternatives if possible. And trending downward
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 8 reviews — take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: He's much more of a "discovery" method teacher.  He will give you partial information and then homework assignments which force you to "discover" the answer.  In other classes, teachers probably tell you this is why this method is better than that and then do the math proof for you.  In this class you figure it out  yourself.

Boring teacher, his mind wanders off in the middle of lectures a lot, meaning a lot of tangential tidbits of non-relevant facts that makes it nearly impossible to learn from his lectures. His homework approaches are basically irrelevant manual labor. Like if I was teaching somebody math and told them to multiple 2 300 digit numbers by hand.

Uninspiring teacher.  When I dipped my toes back in to graduate school in the 90's, I was recommended to try his network class as the Internet was a hot topic  Did try and attended his class; didn't touch another network class for 15 years.
I took his courses and were a TA twice. His lecture was boring and rarely gets updated.
Boring classes. Good at telling stories but not at teaching. A waste of time.

analysis.name: Kaci Fairchild
analysis.verdict: Most students had a tough time — consider alternatives if possible
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 7 reviews — take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: Dr. Fairchild made it clear that she had no respect for her students during a pandemic. Our feedback was never integrated into the course and instead she dug her heels in. The quizzes included sections that were not in the review slides and we were ultimately set up to fail. The course ran overtime each week and the take home message was: Too bad

Dr. Fairchild sets her students up to fail. She provides study guides for her exams, promises to stick to them, then includes information on non-study-guide included topics. She becomes frustrated when asked certain questions and is generally rude to her students over zoom and email. Avoid taking her class if possible.

Dr. Fairchild is extremely petty. If you piss her off she will include subject matter on tests and quizzes that she did not teach you. She is also generally rude and difficult to work with. Avoid her class if possible.
HORRENDOUS. Actually traumatized by kaci. shes mean, rude, disrespectful, etc. Her class sucks and would avoid at all costs .not sure how she is still even allowed to teach/get hired anywhere.
Horrible, rude, very disrespectful. Gets angry at students who ask questions. Even students with 3.7 GPAs and above got Cs and Ds in this class. One word: RUN!

analysis.name: Guadalupe Valdes
analysis.verdict: Below average reviews — check if this fits your learning style. And trending downward
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 21 reviews — decent sample but could shift

review_highlights.comment: I wish we would have covered models of bilingualism in the United States and elsewhere. In the age of post-globalization, things do change fast , and we should be better informed about super-diversity and language rights.

Ms. Valdes gives good feedback about the content that has to do with English learners. She is less interested or not at all interested in promoting Spanish and the  Spanish speakers.  A real disappointment.
The teaching methodology and the content of some of these Ed. courses are outdated.

I'm not impressed with this prof, because her theories of education are based on anecdotes or personal stories.  There is not anything scientific that as educators we can use when we go to the real world of educational leadership.  A real disappointment.

Very easy classes overall.  Smart students should look for someone else with real content and solid educational theories that could enlighten us in the real world of public education. Easy grader 'cz she does not want to make waves.  But it would be better to make waves and teach something challenging ro begin with.

analysis.name: Mandeep Gill
analysis.verdict: Great professor — tough but students love the teaching. But trending upward recently
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 24 reviews — decent sample but could shift

review_highlights.comment: Not great. This professor really only cares about the most smart students in the class. Topics in lectures are very rushed and professor never checks if we have questions. If you try to talk with him about something, he seems generally rude/not caring. Communicating with the professor is difficult, not many office hours. Would not recommend.

Taken @ U of Minnesota(summer semester). Prof. Gill is an extremely intelligent professor who focuses on what makes space interesting, and was always determined to ensure every student understood any complex topics. He was very accessible when further assistance was needed and is truly passionate about seeing his students succeed. Highly recommend!

AST 1001 Summer term 2024, UMN

Professor Gill was an excellent professor and teacher! He expected students to know the information that was most relevant and useful, but taught both the relevant info and information that was interesting. Overall, an engaging and very informative course. Was willing to dive into the weeds to explain complex topics!

Dr. Gill is a king. Whether you're looking for an easy science credit or a mesmerising intro to astronomy you will vibe with AST101!! He designs the course material off of what's genuinely interesting to his students, not checking boxes, & he focuses on the cool, big questions about the universe - not math! Always approachable, caring & passionate.

Organization is spectacularly bad. Multiple platforms are attempted to be used to regulate class assignments and grades. Instructor makes things very unclear and does not offer feedback on harshly graded homework assignments. Attendance is mandatory and no high quality recordings are available for those who can't attend.

analysis.name: Sam Hinkie
analysis.verdict: Solid pick — well-liked with reasonable difficulty. And trending downward
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 10 reviews — decent sample but could shift

review_highlights.comment: He is one of the most forward thinking and intelligent professors i have ever had. He was willing to die for the sins of those beneath him.

Professor Hinkie taught me many important life values that I hold dear. He taught me that patience and trust are necessary to achieve any goal in life. He taught me how to maximize the value of any assets in my life. Most importantly, he taught me how to draft the NBA's next dynasty from the ground up. I will forever be grateful to Mr. Sam Hinkie
Thank you Mr. Hinkie for everything you did for Philadelphia basketball... #TrustTheProcess

Professor Hinkie is an absolute legend, as his sacrifices have lifted Philadelphia basketball above .500 again and towards NBA dominance once again, through the assets he helped acquire like Joel Embiid and Ben Simmons. #TrustTheProcess... always.
Hinkie taught me how to process. Lots of Homework though, and ya gotta buy the book. Otherwise, great guy!!!!! Take him for Process101

analysis.name: Horn Margo
analysis.verdict: Mixed reviews — experiences vary a lot between students
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 4 reviews — take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: Kind of outdated in perspectives and teachings -- you can provide evidence for some things that you interpret from the reading and it doesnt feel welcome. She also forgets things easily which makes it tough to streamline conversations
She's a really nice person and she encourages people to share their ideas, even if she doesn't agree with them.
she's too outdated and isn't open to hearing new ideas even when backed by multiple students and text
A lovely person; she gives you a lot of space to express and discuss your own ideas

analysis.name: Herb Clark
analysis.verdict: Below average reviews — check if this fits your learning style

review_highlights.comment: Went in to talk with him during office hours. Very frustrating, no attempt to actually engage in a real conversation, very arrogant. I would put this class in the bottom 10% of the classes I've had in my major.
Could be an interesting subject, but the lectures are really boring.  Not really a good teacher.
lectures were sooo boring and his notes were incomprehensible; painful class
skip this prof, if at all possible

analysis.name: Donna Hunter
analysis.verdict: Good teacher but expect a challenge

review_highlights.comment: Just terrific, a role model for all of us

Donna is enthusiastic and knowledgeable. She engages the class in the already-interesting discussion of the meaning of criminality. Her extension policy is great; she cares more about your learning than school policy.
Does not honor accommodations and there is so much unnecessary work.
Always gives extensions, no matter what!

an Amazing Professor! Certainly the best professor I ever had.
Morris is awesome. Take this class you'll love it.
An amazing, riveting lecturer who does cool stuff
Very entertaining and very focused on his work

analysis.verdict: Good teacher but expect a challenge. And trending downward

review_highlights.comment: Lecture goes fast . . . he really knows what he is talking about.  Very hard class, but he is really good at office hours.
gautam is the man...plus he writes random linux utilities that make things transparent

analysis.name: Jeff Radwell
analysis.verdict: Mixed reviews — experiences vary a lot between students
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 3 reviews — take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: lectures were very interesting. some material can be difficult but he is accessible and will take time to explain. on top of that, i had a difficult semester and he was the most caring professor i have ever taken. i hope he's doing okay and returns next semester. take him, he is amazing and so is the class.

Professor Radwell had a fast-paced, challenging course. My learning style didn't really match his teaching style, and he was a little ramble-y, but he was so great in every other aspect I had to give him 5 stars.
Knows a LOT about tumors, gives very interesting lectures. The exams are impossibly long, and pretty hard.

analysis.name: Jeff Ullman
analysis.verdict: Below average reviews — check if this fits your learning style
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 12 reviews — decent sample but could shift

review_highlights.comment: Very difficult to deal with. At times incomprehensible. Not useful to students. Preoccupied w/ other (non-academic) matters and issues.
READS OUT NOTES. Fairly useless to listen to his lectures.

Took the Stanford course in high school and loved it. All the complaints about his online course of from people who can't learn unless they pay thousands of dollars to a school to contrive some pressure to learn. All reviews are not in good faith, this is a TURING AWARD winner providing free material online so be more appreciative.
He does not make eye contact and reads from the note. It's just so boring.

Jeff Ullman is a fantastic human being, and he is an expert in his field.  He literally invented some of the algorithms that he teaches in his class (he wrote the textbook, too), and he peppers his lectures with interesting "war stories."  He is also a great research mentor and is 10x better than my real PhD advisor.

analysis.name: Jack Rakove
analysis.verdict: Mixed reviews — experiences vary a lot between students. And trending downward
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 15 reviews — decent sample but could shift

review_highlights.comment: By far one of the worst professors I have had at Stanford. If you have any other options - and I do mean any - run!
Every other word that comes out of this mans mouth is an insult to a racial minority. The only thing worse than Rakoves in-class behavior, is the universitys unwillingness to do anything about it.
This man is incoherent, mean-spirited, and does not care if his students learn anything. Any student thinking about taking his courses needs to consider other options

He was my advisor during his first year (1980).  My only two C's came from him. The most articulate lecuture I've ever heard. Just the lectures are worth the prices of admission.  Kind of caught of in grammar and kind of stuffy -- fits that "east coast prep" stereotype. But then again, that's the charm!  Easily my favorite at Stanford.GoCubs RW   R

Dr. Rakove is really one of our best resources at Stanford University.  Between him and Dr. Lawrence Friedman at the Law School, Stanford offers wonderful opportunities for studies in US legal history.  This class was my first inn history, and also a major change of direction in my academic career - persuaded me to abandon chemistry.

analysis.name: John Taylor
analysis.verdict: Most students had a tough time — consider alternatives if possible
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 3 reviews — take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: John Taylor is well-known in the field of economics. However, hes very old and the lectures are quite dry. No need to attend lectures at all, as the slides are all uploaded and he provides little explanation on top of the info already presented in the slides. Would probably skip any quarter with John Taylor and wait for a diff professor.
He doesn't answer questions in a satisfactory manner. He doesn't explain any concepts at all. Basically, you have to read the textbook and figure it all out yourself.
The lectures were very long and slow, very difficult tests

analysis.name: Richie Kim
analysis.verdict: Mixed reviews — experiences vary a lot between students. But trending upward recently
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 7 reviews — take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: He genuinely cares about his students. The class is very discussion-based, so feel free to share your thoughts on the topic—it's a safe space to express ideas. He's a philosopher at heart, and most topics challenge us to rethink how we live and approach education in the real world.
He claims to always be available to help students; however, instead of helping, he criticizes or confuses them. He is super passive-aggressive. I do not recommend taking his class.

Can seem arrogant one day one or two of class, but a week in and he is one of the best humanity professors I've ever had. Lectures are extremely fun, and he really cares for his students. Although, his courses wont be easy, expect second year STEM class level of difficulty (comparable to Ochem, Calc 3, cell phisiology, etc)
Really enjoyed the class and has a good teaching style
Great Professor!, the readings are tough but the assignments are very straight forward, discussion posts along with a couple of open note workbooks and a final paper, I would recommend his class.

analysis.name: Matthew Kanan
analysis.verdict: Mixed reviews — experiences vary a lot between students
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 3 reviews — take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: He needs to stick to the higher level classes, because he seemed to not be able to understand why we couldn't understand. The more he talked the less I understood.

analysis.name: Keith Schwarz
analysis.verdict: Good teacher but expect a challenge. And trending downward
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 26 reviews — decent sample but could shift

review_highlights.comment: Keith Schwarz is an extremely talented CS teacher. He is organized, clear, and loves his material.  His lectures inspire you to love computer theory too.  But he and his TAs have an extremely punitive grading  style that makes one dread this course. Learning should not be punishment. Computer Science should not be about gatekeeping.
a well meaning teacher, totally unfair class. proofs graded subjectively. TAs and teacher contradict each other and get salty when you ask questions.
Keith's great and this material is super cool! Just too much of it to learn all at once.
Good professor, but makes nearly any class ultra hard

I took CS143, Compilers, in 2011.  Keith was very enthusiastic, very well-prepared, and could answer any question anyone threw at him.  He kept the course on track and covered a lot of material in short time.  He was very helpful in his office hours and just generally a good guy.

analysis.name: Pamela Karlan
analysis.verdict: Below average reviews — check if this fits your learning style. But trending upward recently
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 3 reviews — take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: The haters have got to be MAGA trolls. Seriously one of the most respected profs in the history of SLS. So hilarious, and so incredibly smart. I love that she respects opinions she does not agree with. Never pass up a chance to take a class with Karlan. You will learn so much that you won't even care what grade you get.

Constitutional Litigation spring 2018. She peddled a skewed and twisted approach to law. No blind justice here. Poisonous presentation of the curriculum. If you can avoid this professor, avoid her at all cost. The law school employees several professors like her. In retrospect, I should have gone to a different law school. Shame on Stanford.
If you aren't aligned with her political beliefs and her interpretation of the Constitution, lie. That's the only way to pass this narcissist's class.

analysis.name: Kathleen Tarr
analysis.verdict: Most students had a tough time — consider alternatives if possible
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 3 reviews — take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: Easily the worst litterature instructor I've had. Targets students based on personal bias, abuses authority, and piles on absurd workloads far beyond other PWR classes. Hostile, arrogant, unprofessional. If you value fairness or basic respect, avoid her at all costs.
Do not take this Professor willingly; I was not given proper accommodations despite having a mental health concern. I was forced to present to the whole class when she knew I had not finished my RBA.

Dr. Tarr is both strict and unaccommodating, and exceptions are rarely made. Nearly half of my class this quarter dropped, even as late into the quarter as week seven. If you care about your GPA, your mental health, or your ability to get something meaningful out of your PWR course, please do not take this class.

analysis.name: Kenneth Marshall
analysis.verdict: Mixed reviews — experiences vary a lot between students. And trending downward
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 4 reviews — take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment:
Took his value investing course, overall his approach is methodical and boring filled with using spreadsheets. He also has a big ego when we question his methods. 

His stock picks are mediocre.

Highly recommended. Marshall teaches an online course in Stanford's Continuing Studies program. Knowledgeable, empathetic, and highly motivating. This course was not inexpensive, but it was a good value.

Worth it. First investing course for me. Not a math person. This class made a lot of what used to confuse me about money very understandable. Prof listens well and makes good remarks that help to make the material easier to understand. It's an internet course, but it felt closer than that. The book is very good.
Great online class that was recommended to me from another web site.

analysis.name: Vadim Khayms
analysis.verdict: Great professor — tough but students love the teaching. And trending downward
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 24 reviews — decent sample but could shift

review_highlights.comment: Hilarious guy with dry Russian wit, but also fair and caring.  Decent lecturer with very good course readers (no note-taking!).  Do your own reading in textbook and  course reader or you won't be able to work the HW problems.  The CME 104 text (Greenberg) is excellent and covers CME 100/102/104 material; buy it before CME 100 and thank me later.
He has funny jokes and the curve in the class is insanely good. We love Vadim!
Vadim is awesome! His class is really hard, but everything you do is useful and helps you learn, and he's very clear at explaning things, and quite funny in a deadpan way too!
His classes are the hardest I've ever taken, but they really are the most rewarding. You will walk out of that class knowing all the material, and it will stay with you
Smartest professor with an excellent balance of humor and full grasp of the course.

analysis.name: Leonard  Susskind
analysis.verdict: Solid pick — well-liked with reasonable difficulty. And trending downward
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 5 reviews — take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: Very relatable. Makes Quantum Physics accessible to the common person
One of the best professors and physicists in the world now.

analysis.name: Percy Liang
analysis.verdict: Below average reviews — check if this fits your learning style
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 8 reviews — take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: Difficult to talk to. Makes you feel like asking questions is wasting his time during OH, and that he does not want to be teaching. Otherwise the course was okay, too introductory for modern AI.

Two students from his lab quit during their term because of his constant verbal abuse and harassment. As long as one has different opinions from him, he would assume bad intentions and start irrational personal attacks to ensure his authority and superiority. Conversations are often depressing and toxic. No personal growth of the student victim.

He is the judgemental, controlling, and insensitive professor I have ever seen. He often fails to control his emotion when interacting with others. Public humiliation, yelling, or sarcasm to others happens sometimes. His manner doesn't seem professional and often is considered abusive. Not sure what you can learn given his confusing behavior.

Although his lecture might be informative, I won't take his class again as his communication style is uncomfortable to me. He likes to use intimidation and sometimes jump into conclusion recklessly when communicating with him.

The first half of each lecture is typically an explanation of the concepts, and the second half is done on the whiteboard and/or a live demo on screen. Professor Liang writes code faster than anyone I've ever seen!

analysis.name: Lei Xing
analysis.verdict: Most students had a tough time — consider alternatives if possible
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 4 reviews — take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: Not very clear. Very indirect.
Good thing he doesnt teach that much.
Lei Xing is too old. When he was younger he used to be better but these days he is slow.

analysis.name: Lee Yearley
analysis.verdict: Solid pick — well-liked with reasonable difficulty. And trending downward
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 12 reviews — decent sample but could shift

review_highlights.comment: My favorite undergrad prof. In Religious Studies.  Also my advisor.  He can be a bit intimidating because of his wealth of knowledge, and at times elusive and difficult to get ahold of...however his style and mere presence can enthrall even non-RS students.  A great man if I might, an inestimable influence to me personallym. Check out his seminar!

Prof Yearley is scary sharp. Very hard to argue with. Expert in the history of ideas and Confucian social psychology.  Teaching style: Felt pretentious to me. He relies a lot on metaphors. It disconcerted me sometimes when he alluded to his family/social life or gave out personal advice in class or OH. Sometimes he totally ignored questions.

Simply amazing; personally, my favorite professor of all time.  Brilliant, astoundingly well-read, skilled pedagogue, a little arrogant, but whatever.  You will not only learn a lot about the subject of the class/seminar, but probably about yourself, too.  You owe it to yourself to take at least one of his classes.

Can be aloof and enigmatic, but hes a tremendously perceptive and thoughtful teacher. Worth stopping by his (usually crowded) office hourshe will chat about almost anything and is genuinely interested in his students. His classes on Dante and Zhuangzi are both excellent, well-organized, well-paced.
extremely intelligent and knowledgable, but can seem to be extremely arrogant and cynical. dont let my personal feelings put you off however, cos he's a great professor.

analysis.name: Joshua Landy
analysis.verdict: Great professor — tough but students love the teaching. And trending downward
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 18 reviews — decent sample but could shift

review_highlights.comment: Wonderfully illuminating course. It changed the way I think about the world. After taking the course, everything aesthetic seemed more engaging, complex, and analyzeable. Landy and his co-teacher, Lanier Anderson, are lovely both individually and together.
Brilliant. Articulate. Generous. Funny. Basically, Landy's everything good.

Where do I start?.. Prof. Landy is wonderful in so many ways, and I sincerely hope that, just because I find him extremely attractive, it won't minimize his professionalism, his excellent teaching, his great scholarship, or the fact that he's just a wonderful human being. Unfortunately, I have to stress again: he is HOT!

Landy is probably the most charismatic professor I've ever had. He's funny in a witty, self-deprecating kind of way, and seems genuinely interested in what students have to say. This makes him very approachable. He's also obviously very smart, and his views on the interaction of philosophy and literature are interesting.
sooooooo amazing! one of the kindest and most knowledgeable professors I've ever had. his lectures are so engaging and fun. he is always available if you have any questions or anything.

analysis.name: John L'Heureux
analysis.verdict: Good teacher but expect a challenge. And trending downward
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 12 reviews — decent sample but could shift

review_highlights.comment: He was my prof of modern American poetry at Tufts. I thought he was absolutely extraordinary and now, years later, I realize how great his influence was on me and what a wonderful teacher he was.  I still remember how well he read Yeats.

Took a sophomore seminar on contemporary American short fiction. One of the best classes I've ever taken. Very hard, but the expansion of my views on literature was amazing. Extremely challenging, in a good way.
He is lovely and was a great teacher. I would retake him if possible.
Years ago I took a sophomore seminar from him. Amazing class. Came back here 13 years later just to rave. I can't recommend him highly enough.
He's completely different one-on-one than he is in lecture, and still great. Doesn't take himself too seriously. Take his classes.

analysis.name: Michelle Landis-Dauber
analysis.verdict: Most students had a tough time — consider alternatives if possible
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 13 reviews — decent sample but could shift

review_highlights.comment: Professor Dauber was a very difficult professor in a lot of ways. She was very unavailable and the way she lectured left a lot of questions to be asked. This made the material very difficult to fully grasp and her exams very difficult. I would not take any of her classes again.
So many lectures, very awkward prof. Makes the materials harder than neccessary, doesnt work well with students.

Very unpleasant. Her attitude leaves much to be desired. I love school and I get excellent grades this is the first C I got since 5th grade. It ruined my GPA and she ruined any fun I may have had taking this class. Do NOT waste money taking her class. You'll regret it.

Unfortunately I have found Michelle Landis-Dauber quite a challenge, I found in some aspects it difficult to follow and understand the material in front of us. 

The course is challenging enough, and throwing a challenging lecturer in makes it more so.
Does not engage. Uncaring. Cold. Made a class I looked forward to incredibly unpleasant.

analysis.name: Matt Nash
analysis.verdict: Most students had a tough time — consider alternatives if possible
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 6 reviews — take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: Matt Nash is s_ck. He has been using international students for his own fantasies. Just like other ab_usve men in power. If you are an International student, you will be trapped by this man. He has no clear rubrics for grading exams. It is all subjective. He does this on purpose to select certain students to go to his office for their private time.

If you are an international student first year, just avoid Matt Nash. You might get trapped like I did. II got stuck. I could not complain about his inappropriate behavior with me and I could not drop his class. Even after the class was over, I could not get away until I left the country. He is so powerful that he could destroy my life.

No clarity on grading. He does this on purpose for students to beg him. His grading is random. No guidance or explanation on weight for questions or why he deducts points. I would never take a class for him again ever. I also learned something about him but I ca not talk about it here until official investigation takes place.

Simply put Matt Nash is a p_rv. add to that incompetence and toxicity. He is well-known to take advantage of intentional students for personal private times. We know it. Many told me about it. a few recorded him but they are afraid because they might lose their visa.
Matt Nash is a powerful whi_e man who has no accountability for anything. He has been expl0iting international students for private times in his office. everyone knowns. Nothing happened so far.

review_highlights.comment: None to good. Good for insomnia.
lectures are totally useless, you essentially have to teach yourself everything
A horrible teacher, just sits there and clicks through inscrutable powerpoint slides.  Avoid him at all costs.
Lectures are so boring and useless, no point in going to class.
He was the worst teacher I have ever had.

analysis.name: Nick Parlante
analysis.verdict: Great professor — tough but students love the teaching
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 45 reviews — fairly reliable

review_highlights.comment: He is awesome! He helped me cross the  mental barrier I had towards Computer Science and has created an interest in me for Programming. He is great! He is the Best! Thank you Sir!

He made CodingBat website. Its amazing how his efforts helped thousands of students taking computer science like the AP Computer Science to other college intro to CS (Java) courses. Also thanks to Stanford Professor Marty Stepp.

Nick Parlante is user-friednly!. He is a brilliant lecturer, funny, and easy to listen to. Has an incredible ability to present complex material in clear, concise, and engaging manner. My mind is blown!
I knew nothing when I started taking his courses online. He made everything crystal clear and everything seemed easier. He is perfect at what he does. I thank him with all my heart.
I'm an Asian from and living in a non English speaking country and he could make me understand the whole concept, how cool is that?!

analysis.name: Oyekunle Olukotun
analysis.verdict: Mixed reviews — experiences vary a lot between students

review_highlights.comment: Knowledgeable and completely apathetic.  I would ask him very fundamental questions via email and he would never reply.  The only way to get a response seemed to be call or walk to his office directly.  His lectures were good but then he was off the clock.  He is probably tenured.
Funny to read comments like "He is probably tenured" for the father of multiprocessors...

analysis.name: Brad Osgood
analysis.verdict: Solid pick — well-liked with reasonable difficulty. And trending downward
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 9 reviews — take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: Brad is really such a nice guy and always willing to explain concepts further. Go and visit him during office hours too, because he writes a lot and moves fast in lecture. Would recommend!

One of the best lectures I've ever had at Stanford. Not only did he explain concepts (and pitfalls) clearly, but the lecture notes were very well-written and organized. More importantly, his lectures and notes motivated me to learn by giving interesting applications to Fourier Transform. To top that off, he was a really humorous person.
really cool prof. clear teaching. intresting classes, but lots of homeworks.
Very good big picture explanations. Great notes. Good sense of humor.
Excellent instructor with a great writing style. His notes bring great clarity to the subject of signal processing. Also has a good sense of humor.

review_highlights.comment: If you go to the first lecture and read the books you're in the clear.
Prof is hot! She must buy her outfits in Paris! Font of knowledge.
amazingly current social movement information
Amazing and engaged teacher. I loved this class. I took her grad level course in the 1990s. Still remember it.

analysis.name: Richard Ontiveros
analysis.verdict: Solid pick — well-liked with reasonable difficulty. And trending downward
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 5 reviews — take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: I thought this was an easy "A", boy was I wrong. He kicked everyone's butt and was very good. He could be more detailed but I guess if he did it would be a long class.
He is great. I have never had so much fun learning music history. I didn't I would like it and it was a blast. He was strict but he earned everyone's respect.
He is great. I have never had so much fun learning music history. I didn't I would like it and it was a blast. He was strict but he earned everyone's respect.
He is great. I have never had so much fun learning music history. I didn't I would like it and it was a blast. He was strict but he earned everyone's respect.
Doc O. was awasome. He was very harsh sometimes but he did the job. I was not sure about him because he was so young but he lived up to the expectations.

analysis.name: Philip Zimbardo
analysis.verdict: Mixed reviews — experiences vary a lot between students. And trending downward
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 30 reviews — decent sample but could shift

review_highlights.comment: A mixed bag.  Zimbardo is entertaining and insightful, but also highly egocentric and arrogant.  Seems more interested in demonstrating how brilliant he is than in really teaching—more of a showman than an educator.

I got the opportunity to work with Dr. Zimbardo during the summer one year and it truly changed my life... for the worse. I got to experience first hand how power corrupts people (including Dr. Zimbardo himself). He is not necessarily a bad professor, but he is an evil one. Also doesn't accept illness as a reason to leave experiments early.

Professor Zimbardo was my professor many years ago.  Pretty well-recognized in his field.  Interesting lectures.  Ran research testing students.  Still recall being a research subject as pretty traumatic.  Don't do that.
Kind of full of himself, but hell, he's the****.
Zimbardo is cocky and full of himself.  He thinks that he is the god of psychology, but he is not a very good teacher.

analysis.name: Steven Roberts
analysis.verdict: Excellent choice — highly rated and manageable workload. And trending downward
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 103 reviews — this is a reliable picture

review_highlights.comment: Professor Roberts is truly amazing! He takes the time to get to know his students and wants you to take away the most from his classes. His How to Make a Racist class was extremely impactful in my experience. If you have the opportunity, I 10/10 recommend taking one of his classes!
Amazing teacher and an amazing course!! Material was conveyed very well and Professor Roberts created a very inclusive and caring environment for the class. 10/10 would take a class from him again.

Professor Roberts is the most incredible teacher I have ever learned from. His lectures are captivating, and he cultivates an engaging and interactive learning environment. He made a three hour Monday morning class feel like 45 minutes. I wish I had more space for detail, but his class and teaching truly changed my life. Please take his class.

1) Steven is amazing, just as a person. He is open and friendly. He really cares about his students and our learning experience in his class. And he's not hard on the eyes. 2) He's incomparable as a teacher. He orients it and balances the psychological side of things with relating it to personal experiences. 3) The class topic will change your life

Professor Roberts is very intelligent, and his class has inspired me to become more engaged with the world to make a positive impact. He's considerate and cares about all of his students equally. His approach to the content of class is thoughtful and his talks are well-prepared and intentional. His teaching style makes it easy for anyone to learn.

analysis.name: Tim Roughgarden
analysis.verdict: Good teacher but expect a challenge. And trending downward
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 19 reviews — decent sample but could shift

review_highlights.comment: I took both Algorithm courses on Coursera last year. I think Prof. Roughgarden is one of the best teachers I've ever had. He explained clearly and in detail quite difficult topics. And he's tons of fun too! I'm going to take both courses again next year. It's the kind of experience worth repeating!

The second half of the course is way tougher: heck, that was what I petitioned for and what I got: :-) Some people said that was brains against the wall. Very good course though. The professor is inspirational. The homework is EXTREMELY well thought through, and I am a 30 year veteran. There was absolutely no hands holding, a SUPER plus for me.
Lectures are energetic but he doesn't actually explain anything... I had to learn everything from the textbook. Not helpful at office hours (when he actually showed up for them)

The intuition he builds provides a solid foundation to grasp the mathematically rigorous proof of concepts with ease. He provides great insight into why things are the way they are and kindles huge interest in the subject. Often leaves logical holes in his explanations for you to fill in, which are just within your reach thus will engage you well.
One of the most clear, engaging, interesting teachers I've ever had. I've learned a ton and have enjoyed it.

analysis.name: Robert Weisberg
analysis.verdict: Good teacher but expect a challenge. And trending downward
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 5 reviews — take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: Brilliant and kind. Totally interesting lectures. Sparks intellectual curiosity! What an incredible human.

One of the best professors at SLS; only caveat is an occasional tendency to ramble and then have to rush through material to make up for it, but the rambling is almost always entertaining and informative in it's own right. Extraordinarily approachable both in and out of class- a must-take for anyone interested in criminal law.

Great professor.  Funny, really into the material.  And cares about students and teaching a LOT.  Not always the clearest at explaning things, and assigns a lot of reading.  But more than makes up for it with his intelligence, wit, and dedication to teaching.

Look up mensch in the OyED to find his picture.  Old School in the sense that his progressive politics masks a reactionary streak.  But what a great intellect and fine person even if he is caught up in the institutional politics.  Great teacher but sometimes too disparaging of  economics in attempt to uplift law and humanities.

analysis.name: Steve Rock
analysis.verdict: Below average reviews — check if this fits your learning style
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 7 reviews — take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: I was hoping to learn a lot from this class but trying to get clear explanations from this guy is next to impossible!
Barely introduces topics, and then assigns problems that require he taught well (which is rarely the case).

Handwrites everything on the board (No slides).
Notes are riddled with mistakes.
Misleading diagrams.
He never displays MATLAB plots in class.
Topics are random with no logical flow.
He is impatient with students' concerns.
You have to listen to each lecture video three times to understand.
HW not worded clearly and hard to match with lecture notes

I have to agree with the other guy.  Its weird he makes simple topics like conservation of momentum or energy seem way harder and complex then they should be.  Grading in fine and fair...but don't expect to be wowed by him
Well-meaning, but not very clear.  Ironically, has difficulty teaching the fundamentals on which he likes to focus.  Tests are OK.

analysis.name: Sean Szumlanski
analysis.verdict: Mixed reviews — experiences vary a lot between students. And trending downward
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 9 reviews — take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: He provides 0 context before doing any programming. He give off the appearance of constant busyness and pompous self-important while secretly resenting students. He sometimes always talks about being alone and makes inappropriate off-topic jokes about tech layoffs and competitive programming. Lastly, his exams are more difficult than lectures.

I have NEVER loved a teacher more than Sean. I took CS106B as a rising high school senior during the 2024 Summer Quarter, and even though he had to rush through some material (especially once we got to advanced data structures), I always looked forward to his lectures! He's energetic, concise, takes questions... Sean could make ANY class worth it!

If you're new to programming, don't listen to him. He said "DSA" should be named "Thinking clearly", and he'd advice against looking up solutions even if you've spent more than 30 min on a problem and couldn't find a solution. Your brain is a neural network, and you need to repeat things, and making mistakes teaches you the best. Repitition is key.

He's the GOAT and, trust me, I've had a lot of good professors. Ignore the 1/5 ratings that one (probably salty) student has been drowning him with. I'm not sure where they're coming from with claiming he doesn't help them succeed in courses because dude constantly goes above and beyond on being available outside of class. Best lectures of all time

People come here for feedback, so I want to say that he is a truly phenomenal but challenging professor who is amazing at teaching, he even brought homemade cookies to class once. There is exactly one dedicated person who has given him 40-50 negative reviews on all sorts of websites over the last three years, so I wanted to give a warning for that.

analysis.name: Ruth Starkman
analysis.verdict: Great professor — tough but students love the teaching. And trending downward
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 23 reviews — decent sample but could shift

review_highlights.comment: Professor Starkman is a little intimdating. She's very welcoming in class and encouraging. Then she rips your work to shreds. Literally. Every. Word. But then she shows you how to improve and she makes you want to do better. In the end you do OK, make friends in class, and learn a lot.
Energetic lectures on tech and ethics, clearly loves her subject. But be prepared. She'll walk up to you and ask a question about Kant or code or ?? Starkman is the OG.

Here's the thing  with Professor Starkman. She's super smart and has zero patience for dogma of any kind. Don't just come in and spout off your private theory of ethics. You will just embarrass yourself. The reading is hard . You need to do it before class and be prepared. See her in her office hours, she's not that intimidating in person.

This woman is like a reincarnation of a GOAT, she is literally an OG at everything. Hands down, one of the best profs I've ever had. The campus literally revolves around her. However, sometimes her class can get difficult, but it really do be like that sometimes. Either way, she helps you understand it; most importantly, she actually cares for you!

The stuff I learned with Professor Starkman has stayed with me for years, and when you go back to visit she remembers you perfectly like no time has gone by. Ask her for help on job apps and gradmed school, nobody goes the extra mile like she does.

analysis.name: Mike Tomz
analysis.verdict: Good teacher but expect a challenge. And trending downward
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 14 reviews — decent sample but could shift

review_highlights.comment: Awesome teacher. When I first took this class, I thought I would be turned off from IR. How wrong I was. Besides that he's an influential guy in the field, he's extremely clear and helpful. The tests are hard and you have to do a lot of work, but it's worth it.

Awesome professor, had him over the summer for 8 weeks, lots of reading to prepare for the lectures, quite a few papers, but overall, 100% worth it. While he might assign a lot of reading, all of it helps you understand the material much better. The papers are assigned to make sure you're paying attention in lecture and understand the material.
Excellent class; its painful during the process but it provides a foundation for any PoliSci class you'll take after it.

Arrogant weasel, writes 'gotcha' exams (relevance of Annie Oakley to international relations?  if she'd accidentally shot some German politician, WWI might not have happened....  Is this really something that should be on a test?), uses and abuses his research assistant gophers.
Very knowledgeable and confident. Always kind and happy to answer questions. Leave room for discussions and always stop class early to interact with students.

analysis.name: Thomas Beischer
analysis.verdict: Good teacher but expect a challenge. And trending downward
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 5 reviews — take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: Great selection of readings that are a fantastic foundation for a decent understanding of architecture theory, expects the best but provides support to get there, great at coaxing out great discussion

Ended up writing a paper I was proud of and have used for writing samples since, went to several office hours meetings and wrote multiple drafts that received feedback. A lot of independence and work was expected but when he saw that effort was being put in he was a huge help
Super friendly and really cares about his students. Though there is a lot of material in the class, it can be managed. You get what you put into this class.
Really pushes you to your potential, expects improvement over the duration of the course, gives great feedback when sought out. Self motivation is a must

analysis.name: Ravi Vakil
analysis.verdict: Solid pick — well-liked with reasonable difficulty
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 4 reviews — take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: very inspiring professor! You can feel in every class that he loves his field and enjoys sharing his passion for mathematics with his students.

analysis.name: Jacob Vo
analysis.verdict: Good teacher but expect a challenge. And trending downward
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 36 reviews — fairly reliable

review_highlights.comment: He has a lot of personality to showcase. He will make you work for your grade and don't think about skipping class. He's very knowledgeable with the materials but I wish he would give less work.
Interesting course to take. Probably won't take it again since he mostly relies on his TA's to grade and answer discussion questions.

He is by far one of the nicest and youngest professors I've had. His lecture slides were amazing and he seemed to be very passionate about Education. To those that are planning to take his class, be prepared to work hard. Overall, he's an amazing professor.
He assigned way too much work for an undergrad class but overall, he's pretty chill. I wouldn't take his class again because I don't have to. It's easy to pass if you put in the work.
Probably one of the youngest professors I've had so far. He has an awkward personality, which makes him really cute and personable. Highly recommend taking his class.

analysis.name: Blakey Vermeule
analysis.verdict: Mixed reviews — experiences vary a lot between students. But trending upward recently
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 3 reviews — take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: Prof. Vermeule  is a space cadet. When she isn't looking at her reflection, she is very self-absorbed and at times less than lucid. Overall, an easy class. I'm not certain this class topic was the best fit for her.

Really interesting professor. I personally liked Adam's contributions more (he is a Pulitzer Prize winner who teaches 49 along with Blakey), but she was pretty funny and engaging. Not an English/humanities major but really enjoyed the class.

Awesome professor. She cares about student's opinions and makes the class into an open conversation rather than a strict lecture which encourages intellectual growth. Definitely reccomend taking one of her courses.

analysis.name: Vladislav Kargin
analysis.verdict: Mixed reviews — experiences vary a lot between students. But trending upward recently
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 3 reviews — take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: This is a great professor. Although he is hard to understand, he really cares about his students and does everything to help them. Take this class its really awesome!!!
Professor Kargin is almost impossible to understand. The class is terrible. I haven't learned a thing from lecture, just from the book and section.
I really recommend this class. Its so great! Professor Kargin is an amazing teacher and has so much to share! Best class at Stanford

analysis.name: Wojciech Wieczorek
analysis.verdict: Below average reviews — check if this fits your learning style. But trending upward recently
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 5 reviews — take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: I have never left a class thinking to myself "I did not learn a single thing." It was such an abysmal time, everyone in the class was beyond confused by the material. The Professor simply did not teach the material properly at all.
Esta clase es dura, pero en el buen sentido.
Poor teacher, does not explain concepts well

One of the worst professors I've had at Stanford. On top of giving hard psets (even during the week we have a midterm), his lectures are hard to follow because his method of teaching is different from the textbook. It's gotten to a point where I've had to teach myself the content.

analysis.name: Marius Wernig

review_highlights.comment:
When his students got offer, he did not congratulate but tell them they not qualify.
He is a kid, dry, cold, heartless, childish, irresponsible, selfish, short-vision, non-communicated, bossing.Most of his project from his idea are not work, but he ask you to do it, not listen.
He is a liar. He lie about his idea is great idea, promise but can not

He still ask students to work for him after they left his lab. 
He would not able to provide research funding as he promised when you join his lab. He did not allow you to buy research reagents and use the plasmid he gave but not work.
He is very rude when he talk you in his office, but in public, polite, he is extreme hypocritical. slave student

He did not alllow the india postdoc to do his own project, instead of ask him to help do  electrophysiology experiment to contribute another postdoc from Europe
He underestimate and insult students, never give positive feed back, never encourage.He did not allow students to apply fellowship, and not gave recommendation letter He use student as tool

more of time people in his lab are not happy.

19. science should be flexible. Jump to a conclusion too quick.
