analysis.name: Tobias Wolff
analysis.verdict: Solid pick — well-liked with reasonable difficulty. And trending downward
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 6 reviews — take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: I took this class spring 1999. Prof. Wolff was the kindest teacher I've ever had; he helped me through a really rough time.  He showed me that it doesn't take much to connect with another human being.  I have since graduated and teach English at another college.  He is my role model. I will always remember his kindness to me during that time.
they made a movie about this guy on lifetime and he has a definition on wikipedia.. take him
prof wolff was so nice he is the kindest professor I've had so far and he additionally teaches and displays the English curriculum very well and is understandable. would definitely take again.
His lectures were interesting, and original. While he was kind, he had a bit of an ego. Overall, I would take another class with him.
Relatable. Awesome. Take this course!

analysis.name: Brian White
analysis.verdict: Solid pick — well-liked with reasonable difficulty. And trending downward
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 6 reviews — take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: Crystal clear teacher. Never saw a teacher with such clear teaching in class.

Prof. White is the BEST.  Incredibly smart and can lecture without using any notes.  His speaking style is such that you're always engaged.  I took his class as an EE major and almost switched to math.  He's really friendly in office hours and very personable too if you ever invite him to faculty night.  Prof. White is a real treasure.

Professor White does a good job of clearly explaining mathematical concepts from a technical perspective, taking care to prove each of his theorems as he teaches. Easily one of the most popular professors of introductory math classes. Plus, he wears crazy Hawaiian shirts.
Best teacher I've had in the math department--no surprise he's the only lecturer you're allowed to have if you're in ACE (accelerated calculus for engineers.) Clear, fair, and makes it interesting
absolutely amazing. Everyone in every other lecture for math 51 this term ended up in professor white's lecture because he is simply that good.

analysis.name: Keith Winstein
analysis.verdict: Below average reviews — check if this fits your learning style. But trending upward recently
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 5 reviews — take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: I'd heard great things about this course in the past, this instructor is OK, but I'm not learning much about all the policy and ethics I'd heard about.
This class was OK, easy, not a lot of work or thinking, I was really hoping for more ideas and approaches to deal with the problems of tech, being a developer, and Silicon Valley
Really interesting class. Winstein really cares about helping you learn, and as long as you engage with lectures and go to lab sessions, you'll do fine in the class.
Disorganized and unable to convey the primary learning goals of his class.
GODSEND. One of the best instructors @ Stanford

analysis.name: William Petig
analysis.verdict: Good teacher but expect a challenge. And trending downward
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 6 reviews — take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: Amazing teacher, funny and entertaining but also the best teacher in the German Department. You will learn your stuff without even noticing it. Not hard or strict but you still learn and have fun. Its never hard going to class, there is never a dull moment. Very good at explaining. Best Professor at Stanford.

Prof. Petig is one of Stanford's gems! He's truly one of the best professor's I've ever had. He is extremely smart and caring - which makes for a great combination for a student to learn languages. He is one of my favorite professors and I learned more than German with shin, I've learned things that have been important for life. Bravo Herr Petig!

One of my favorite profs!!!  Insanely funny, and always very helpful.  Willing to meet, plans out of class events for fun, and  always has hilarious stories and sayings.  If you see the name Petig, take the class.  Grading is very fair, not easy, but def. not hard.  If you give effort, you'll earn your A.
Petig is one of the finest teachers I've ever had.  He keeps class extremely entertaining, but keeps you on your toes and makes you work hard.  Very clear explanations.
Continuing studies course (not sure it is actually '01'): very friendly and approachable, planned gatherings outside of the class too, really liked it.

analysis.name: Thomas Weber
analysis.verdict: Below average reviews — check if this fits your learning style. And trending downward
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 4 reviews — take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: He has a preoccupation with notation that I felt was not necessary for this level of course (save it for 341!). His lecture notes were inconsistent at times with his board writing, and there was no text to reinforce the concepts, which were explained poorly in class. I will avoid Weber for any future classes unless they're required for my degree.
This was a cool course. Lots of practical applications! I would recommend this if you are willing to put in some time. Prof has funny stories. Drawback: course only offered for 3 units.
One of the worst professors I've had. His final was so hard and poorly written, that he said "If you understood it completely, you should be teaching the class"

analysis.name: Patrick Young
analysis.verdict: Solid pick — well-liked with reasonable difficulty. And trending downward
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 9 reviews — take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: Tries to make lectures entertaining, but because they are online there is little incentive to go.

Really enjoyed this class, Patrick really makes sure that you understand the material. He wrote the book, so he lectures in a similar "straight forward" manner. Very nice guy, more people should laugh at his dry humor.

Extremely friendly once you get to know him. Come to his office hour and chat with him. Knowledgable and understand what it's like to be an undergraduate. Great lecturer. His exams can be challenging but study and you'll be fine.
Dr. Young was fantastic. A little boring in class and can be a drag at times, but he is clearly passionate about your success and help you out.
Dr. Young is way cool. CS105 changed my life. Made me want to drop out of school and just design webpages.

analysis.name: Dimitry Yandulov
analysis.verdict: Below average reviews — check if this fits your learning style. And trending downward
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 8 reviews — take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: Excellent knowledge base. Lacks social skills. Whenever someone asks him a question, he ALWAYS answers back with a question. I'm like, "why do I even ask in the first place?" Not helpful. Boring. Too fast. For people who aren't fast learners don't take him.
He is a good person that lives for his work. He needs to enjoy life more. He is very caring under his serious look.

In lab, this Dima is helpful in that he'll help you with setup and understand what is going on. However, in lecture, this guy is really boring to the point where most of the class falls asleep. In fact, I ended up studying in the library rather than attending lecture and my grade improved.

He is the most useful of all the Professors. He actually stays around during class and makes sure you do everything right. I would not want to work for him though because he is such a hard @$$. Everything needs to be done perfectly.

Dima is one of the best three professors in the chem dept.  His social skills are lacking, and he has trouble retaining graduate students, but he does show serious concern about whether his undergrad students are learning.  He came to lab for a couple hours every day, and personally instructed us on lab technique.  A real asset to Stanford chem.

analysis.name: Yitzhak Katznelson
analysis.verdict: Most students had a tough time — consider alternatives if possible
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 3 reviews — take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: This was one of the hardest classes I've taken.  His lectures are worthless cause you can't understand anything he says.  Supposedly he is a genius- but in the research field.  I don't doubt that.  As an educator, he stinks.

Ditto what the other person said.  Clearly he's a world famous researcher.  His teaching though is incomprehensible.  Didn't learn a thing from 113, took 114 with Milgram, and everything suddenly made sense.
His textbook (which he wrote himself) is brilliantly clear. His lectures are incomprehensible. I never understood why there was such a difference.
